fixtureExamples.exampleScenario: Locus status and control panel shows Ardyn review posture as static metadata.
fixtureExamples.shortDescription: Shows review-only Ardyn status without controls.
fixtureExamples.longDescription: Locus may display Ardyn phase status, blocked runtime state, and readiness summaries as static review metadata only.

accessibilityFields.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Expose status text and blocked reason as readable static content; do not attach keyboard activation to blocked controls.

accessibilityNotes.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Expose status text and blocked reason as readable static content; do not attach keyboard activation to blocked controls.

fixtureExamples.allowedDisplayBehavior: Render phase status, current posture, and blocked runtime state as read-only labels.
fixtureExamples.requiredFutureContractBeforeInteractivit: A future Locus control-surface contract plus explicit Ardyn command/runtime authorization.

staticTextRows: Ardyn status and control readiness
Shows review-only Ardyn status without controls.
Review-only metadata. No command, runtime, storage, connector, network, or task behavior is present.
displayFixtureExamplePayload.colorIndependentIndicator: text plus non-color status token required
displayFixtureExamplePayload.hiddenActionPolicy: no auto-execution and no hidden action semantics

fixtureExamples.exampleScenario: Locus review artifact panel summarizes a local Ardyn artifact without controls.
fixtureExamples.shortDescription: Displays review artifacts as non-authorizing evidence.
fixtureExamples.longDescription: Locus may show review artifact summaries, classifications, and rejection reasons without treating them as approvals or grants.

accessibilityFields.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Keep artifact sections navigable by heading and label every rejection reason as text.

accessibilityNotes.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Keep artifact sections navigable by heading and label every rejection reason as text.

fixtureExamples.allowedDisplayBehavior: Render artifact fields, source phase, and non-authorizing proof flags as static evidence.
fixtureExamples.requiredFutureContractBeforeInteractivit: A future human review workflow contract with separate approval-decision and grant phases.

staticTextRows: Review artifact summary
Displays review artifacts as non-authorizing evidence.
Review-only metadata. No command, runtime, storage, connector, network, or task behavior is present.
displayFixtureExamplePayload.colorIndependentIndicator: text plus non-color status token required
displayFixtureExamplePayload.hiddenActionPolicy: no auto-execution and no hidden action semantics

fixtureExamples.exampleScenario: Locus capability metadata panel lists blocked Ardyn capability facts.
fixtureExamples.shortDescription: Shows capability descriptions without invoking tools.
fixtureExamples.longDescription: Locus may display Ardyn capability names, tags, scopes, and blocked authorization state without adapter calls or tool execution.

accessibilityFields.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Expose capability name, status, and blocked reason in the same focus region.

accessibilityNotes.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Expose capability name, status, and blocked reason in the same focus region.

fixtureExamples.allowedDisplayBehavior: Render capability metadata, requested scopes, and blocked execution status.
fixtureExamples.requiredFutureContractBeforeInteractivit: A future capability invocation contract with explicit adapter, connector, MCP/task, and secret boundaries.

staticTextRows: Capability metadata
Shows capability descriptions without invoking tools.
Review-only metadata. No command, runtime, storage, connector, network, or task behavior is present.
displayFixtureExamplePayload.colorIndependentIndicator: text plus non-color status token required
displayFixtureExamplePayload.hiddenActionPolicy: no auto-execution and no hidden action semantics

fixtureExamples.exampleScenario: Locus blocked runtime and command indicator marks execution unavailable.
fixtureExamples.shortDescription: Shows blocked command/runtime status.
fixtureExamples.longDescription: Locus may display that runtime and command exposure are unavailable, including zero-stdout failure expectations.

accessibilityFields.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Announce unavailable status and reason without presenting the indicator as a button.

accessibilityNotes.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Announce unavailable status and reason without presenting the indicator as a button.

fixtureExamples.allowedDisplayBehavior: Render blocked command state and static error semantics.
fixtureExamples.requiredFutureContractBeforeInteractivit: A future command-exposure approval and runtime-enable contract.

staticTextRows: Runtime unavailable
Shows blocked command/runtime status.
Review-only metadata. No command, runtime, storage, connector, network, or task behavior is present.
displayFixtureExamplePayload.colorIndependentIndicator: text plus non-color status token required
displayFixtureExamplePayload.hiddenActionPolicy: no auto-execution and no hidden action semantics

fixtureExamples.exampleScenario: Locus Secure Drop compose and inbox status remains a future metadata placeholder.
fixtureExamples.shortDescription: Shows future compose/inbox status as metadata only.
fixtureExamples.longDescription: Locus may display future Secure Drop compose and inbox placeholders only as unavailable content-fabric capability references.

accessibilityFields.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Label compose and inbox placeholders as unavailable; do not expose file picker or send semantics.

accessibilityNotes.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Label compose and inbox placeholders as unavailable; do not expose file picker or send semantics.

fixtureExamples.allowedDisplayBehavior: Render unavailable compose/inbox metadata and future-contract notes.
fixtureExamples.requiredFutureContractBeforeInteractivit: A future canonical content-fabric Secure Drop contract with separate crypto, transport, inbox, file, connector, and secret authorization.

staticTextRows: Secure Drop placeholder
Shows future compose/inbox status as metadata only.
Review-only metadata. No command, runtime, storage, connector, network, or task behavior is present.

displayFixtureExamplePayload.hiddenActionPolicy: no auto-execution and no hidden action semantics

fixtureExamples.exampleScenario: Multiverse visible AI capability badge marks Ardyn capability as display-only.

fixtureExamples.requiredFutureContractBeforeInteractivit: A future visible capability interaction contract with tool, MCP, connector, and adapter authorization.

fixtureExamples.exampleScenario: Multiverse task or capability wrapper card shows blocked Ardyn task semantics.

accessibilityFields.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Use static region labels for task status; do not hide activation behind card click handlers.

fixtureExamples.allowedDisplayBehavior: Render task wrapper metadata and blocked task runtime state.

fixtureExamples.exampleScenario: Multiverse registry, websocket, MCP, and task indicator shows all runtime surfaces blocked.
fixtureExamples.shortDescription: Shows registry, websocket, MCP, and task runtime blocks.
fixtureExamples.longDescription: Multiverse may display registry, websocket, MCP, and task runtime indicators only as blocked status metadata.

accessibilityFields.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Announce each blocked runtime category explicitly and keep the indicator static by default.

accessibilityNotes.keyboardScreenReaderDisplayNotes: Announce each blocked runtime category explicitly and keep the indicator static by default.

fixtureExamples.allowedDisplayBehavior: Render blocked registry, websocket, MCP, and task runtime categories.

topDisplayFixtureExampleGaps: The example pack is static metadata only; no Locus or Multiverse consumer rendering implementation exists in Ardyn.
No browser, rendering, WCAG automation, visual regression, or screen-reader QA harness exists in Ardyn.
No consumer-owned fixture import contract, conformance runner, or CI handoff exists for these examples.
No interactive approval/control contract exists; examples cannot expose actions, commands, runtime controls, or hidden semantics.
Secure Drop, registry, websocket, MCP, task execution, service discovery, and scheduling remain blocked metadata indicators.
